text.title: Hacker News Launch Strategy Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Hacker News Launch Strategy. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: HN account has ≥4 weeks of genuine comments + karma ≥50 before posting Show HN. Day-zero accounts get auto-flagged.
Title describes what it is, not why it's amazing. No 'revolutionary', 'AI-powered', 'game-changing'.
rule.rationale: Hypey titles get downvoted + flagged within minutes.

rule.statement: Post Tuesday-Thursday 8-10 AM ET. Outside this window front-page traffic halves.
rule.rationale: Off-window posts get <50% of front-page traffic.

rule.statement: Founder is present and replying to every comment for the first 24-48h. Silence kills the thread.
Demo URL works without signup/auth for at least the read-only path. Login walls get flagged.
One Show HN per project; relaunch only after a real version change + ≥30 days.
If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-growth-hacker-news-launch.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
